coaching.title: Easy 8 mi + Strides
coaching.message: Build Phase, Week 11 of 22 — 84 days to Julian Alps. Today's session is 8 miles Zone 2 (HR under 155 bpm, fully conversational) with 4x 20-second strides at the very end. The strides should feel controlled and fast — not all-out — with 60-90 sec walk recovery between each. This is a travel day with a move to Guatemala City this afternoon, so get the run done early. Keep pace honest: the strides add neuromuscular stimulus without digging into your aerobic reserves. Julian Alps demands power hiking efficiency — let your form be deliberate even on flat terrain today.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: For an 8-mile easy run, eat a light carb-forward snack 30-45 min pre-run — banana, toast with honey, or a small bowl of oats (~40-50g carbs). At Zone 2 pace you don't need fuel during the run itself. Post-run recovery window is critical on a travel day: within 30-45 min, hit 60-80g carbs + 25-30g protein (eggs, Greek yogurt, chicken with rice) to lock in adaptation before the transit stress. Stay ahead of hydration throughout the day — altitude and travel dehydrate you faster than you think. Target 3L of water before arriving in Guatemala City.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Travel days create hidden recovery debt — airport stress, sitting, time zone shifts, and inconsistent food all spike cortisol and blunt adaptation. After your run, prioritize 10 min of dynamic mobility (hip circles, leg swings, calf raises) before you pack. During the transit to Guatemala City, get up and walk every 45-60 minutes if possible. Tonight: aim for 8+ hours of sleep in the new location even if the schedule fights you — this is where this week's training consolidates. Keep alcohol off the table tonight; it will trash your sleep quality and slow muscle repair.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're 12 weeks out and in the heart of the build — this is exactly where the race is won or lost. The athletes who toe the line at Julian Alps confident aren't the ones who trained the hardest; they're the ones who were consistent when it was inconvenient. Running 8 miles before packing up and moving cities is a demonstration of the discipline that will get you through 31 miles of Alpine terrain. Bank it. Every low-key, unglamorous easy day stacks into race-day capability. Kranjska Gora is coming — stay the course.
